_comment: Pool of Yankees legends used by Round 4 (Build the Greatest Team). 'wiki' is the English Wikipedia page title; the app fetches a freely-licensed lead photo live via the Wikipedia REST API. Deep positional depth so every slot has many choices. Regenerate blurbs with tools/fetch_content.py.

players.name: Yogi Berra
players.blurb: 3-time MVP, 10 rings — the most in MLB history.

players.name: Bill Dickey
players.blurb: 11-time All-Star catcher; shares retired No. 8 with Berra.

players.name: Thurman Munson
players.blurb: 1976 AL MVP; first Yankees captain since Gehrig.

players.name: Elston Howard
players.blurb: First Black Yankee; 1963 AL MVP catcher.

players.name: Jorge Posada
players.blurb: Core Four catcher, 5-time All-Star, 275 HR.

players.name: Joe Girardi
players.blurb: Caught Cone's perfect game; later a title-winning manager.

players.name: Lou Gehrig
players.blurb: The Iron Horse. 2,130 straight games; AL-record 185 RBI.

players.name: Don Mattingly
players.blurb: Donnie Baseball. 1985 MVP, 9 Gold Gloves.

players.name: Chris Chambliss
players.blurb: His 1976 walk-off pennant sent the Yankees back to the Series.

players.name: Tino Martinez
players.blurb: Dynasty first baseman; a grand slam in the 1998 Series.

players.name: Bill Skowron
players.blurb: “Moose.” Powerful first baseman on five pennant winners.

players.name: Jason Giambi
players.blurb: Middle-of-the-order power in the early-2000s Bronx.

players.name: Wally Pipp
players.blurb: Two-time HR champ — then a headache handed his job to Gehrig.

players.name: Tony Lazzeri
players.blurb: Murderers’ Row second baseman, clutch RBI man.

players.name: Willie Randolph
players.blurb: Steady leadoff second baseman, 6-time All-Star.

players.name: Robinson Cano
players.blurb: Silky-smooth second baseman, 5 Silver Sluggers in NY.

players.name: Joe Gordon
players.blurb: “Flash.” 1942 AL MVP, acrobatic keystone glove.

players.name: Bobby Richardson
players.blurb: The only World Series MVP from a losing team (1960).

players.name: Alfonso Soriano
players.blurb: 40-40 threat at second base in the early 2000s.

players.name: Snuffy Stirnweiss
players.blurb: Won the 1945 AL batting title in the wartime years.

players.name: Alex Rodriguez
players.blurb: 696 career HR; carried the 2009 title run.

players.name: Graig Nettles
players.blurb: Gold Glove wizard; his 1978 Series defense was larceny.

players.name: Red Rolfe
players.blurb: Table-setter at the hot corner for four straight titles.

players.name: Clete Boyer
players.blurb: One of the great defensive third basemen of his era.

players.name: Scott Brosius
players.blurb: 1998 World Series MVP; heart of the dynasty defense.

players.name: Wade Boggs
players.blurb: HOF hitting machine; rode a police horse in the 1996 clinch.

players.name: Derek Jeter
players.blurb: The Captain. 3,465 hits, 5 titles, Mr. November.

players.name: Phil Rizzuto
players.blurb: The Scooter. 1950 AL MVP, then beloved broadcaster.

players.name: Frankie Crosetti
players.blurb: Shortstop-turned-coach who collected 17 rings in pinstripes.

players.name: Tony Kubek
players.blurb: 1957 Rookie of the Year; later an Emmy-winning broadcaster.

players.name: Gil McDougald
players.blurb: 1951 Rookie of the Year who starred at three infield spots.

players.name: Bucky Dent
players.blurb: A light-hitting shortstop who broke Boston’s heart in 1978.

players.name: Rickey Henderson
players.blurb: Set the franchise single-season steals record (93 in 1988).

players.name: Roy White
players.blurb: Switch-hitting lifer who bridged the lean years to the dynasty.

players.name: Charlie Keller
players.blurb: “King Kong.” Feared power bat of the pre-war outfield.

players.name: Bob Meusel
players.blurb: Murderers’ Row slugger with a legendary throwing arm.

players.name: Hideki Matsui
players.blurb: “Godzilla.” 2009 World Series MVP.

players.name: Brett Gardner
players.blurb: Last man standing from the 2009 champs; grinder and pest.

players.name: Joe DiMaggio
players.blurb: The Yankee Clipper. 56-game streak, 9 titles.

players.name: Mickey Mantle
players.blurb: The Mick. Switch-hitting Triple Crown power, 536 HR.

players.name: Bernie Williams
players.blurb: Switch-hitting center fielder, 4 rings, 1998 batting title.

players.name: Earle Combs
players.blurb: Leadoff catalyst of Murderers’ Row, HOF center fielder.

players.name: Bobby Murcer
players.blurb: Homegrown star anointed “the next Mantle.”

players.name: Mickey Rivers
players.blurb: “Mick the Quick.” Leadoff spark of the 1977–78 champs.

players.name: Babe Ruth
players.blurb: The Sultan of Swat. 659 HR as a Yankee; built the dynasty.

players.name: Roger Maris
players.blurb: 61 in ’61 — broke Ruth’s single-season HR record.

players.name: Dave Winfield
players.blurb: Power and a cannon arm; a Hall of Fame all-around bat.

players.name: Paul O'Neill
players.blurb: The Warrior. Heart of the ’90s dynasty, .300 hitter.

players.name: Aaron Judge
players.blurb: All Rise. AL-record 62 HR in 2022, two MVPs.

players.name: Reggie Jackson
players.blurb: Mr. October. Three homers on three pitches in 1977 Game 6.

players.name: Tommy Henrich
players.blurb: “Old Reliable.” Clutch right fielder of the DiMaggio era.

players.name: Hank Bauer
players.blurb: Ex-Marine right fielder; a 17-game World Series hit streak.

players.name: Don Baylor
players.blurb: Fearless middle-order DH who crowded the plate.

players.name: Ron Blomberg
players.blurb: Took the first at-bat as a designated hitter in MLB history.

players.name: Whitey Ford
players.blurb: The Chairman of the Board. .690 career win pct.

players.name: Lefty Gomez
players.blurb: 6-0 in the World Series, ace of the ’30s dynasty.

players.name: Red Ruffing
players.blurb: 273 wins, workhorse of six championship teams.

players.name: Ron Guidry
players.blurb: Louisiana Lightning. 25-3, 1.74 ERA in 1978.

players.name: Andy Pettitte
players.blurb: Core Four lefty; MLB-record 19 postseason wins.

players.name: Mel Stottlemyre
players.blurb: Ace of the lean years; 40 shutouts in pinstripes.

players.name: Allie Reynolds
players.blurb: “Superchief.” Threw two no-hitters in 1951.

players.name: Vic Raschi
players.blurb: “The Springfield Rifle.” Won 21 three years running.

players.name: Waite Hoyt
players.blurb: Hall of Fame ace of the first Yankees dynasty.

players.name: Herb Pennock
players.blurb: “The Knight of Kennett Square.” Crafty HOF lefty.

players.name: Spud Chandler
players.blurb: 1943 AL MVP; owns the lowest ERA in franchise history.

players.name: Catfish Hunter
players.blurb: Baseball’s first big free-agent ace; a Hall of Famer.

players.name: Roger Clemens
players.blurb: The Rocket. Overpowering years anchoring the 2000 champs.

players.name: David Cone
players.blurb: Threw a perfect game on Yogi Berra Day, 1999.

players.name: CC Sabathia
players.blurb: Horse of the 2009 champs; 3,000-strikeout Hall of Famer.

players.name: David Wells
players.blurb: Threw a perfect game in 1998 — by his account, not fully rested.

players.name: Mariano Rivera
players.blurb: The greatest closer ever. 652 saves, unanimous HOF.

players.name: Rich Gossage
players.blurb: Goose. Fireballing HOF closer of the Bronx Zoo.

players.name: Sparky Lyle
players.blurb: First AL reliever to win a Cy Young Award (1977).

players.name: Dave Righetti
players.blurb: Threw a July 4 no-hitter, then set a save record as closer.

players.name: Johnny Murphy
players.blurb: “Fireman.” One of the game’s first great relief aces.

players.name: Aroldis Chapman
players.blurb: Triple-digit heat; closed the 2017–era bullpen.

players.name: John Wetteland
players.blurb: 1996 World Series MVP; saved all four clinchers.
